wild_onion.n.01.definition: widely distributed North American wild onion with white to rose flowers

wild_pea.n.01.definition: a weak-stemmed winter annual native to Mediterranean region for long established in southern United States; cultivated as a cover and pasture crop

wild_plum.n.01.definition: California evergreen wild plum with spiny leathery leaves and white flowers

wild_sheep.n.01.definition: large white wild sheep of northwestern Canada and Alaska

willow.n.01.definition: hybrid willow usually not strongly weeping in habit

willowherb.n.01.definition: tall North American perennial with creeping rootstocks and narrow leaves and spikes of pinkish-purple flowers occurring in great abundance in burned-over areas or recent clearings; an important honey plant

window.n.01.definition: a window of a store facing onto the street; used to display merchandise for sale in the store

windstorm.n.01.definition: a windstorm that lifts up clouds of dust or sand

wisdom.n.01.definition: wisdom that is recondite and abstruse and profound

wolf.n.01.definition: a wolf with a brindled grey coat living in forested northern regions of North America

woman's_clothing.n.01.definition: a loose dressing gown for women

wood.n.01.definition: light easily worked wood of a tulip tree; used for furniture and veneer

wood_fern.n.01.definition: North American fern with evergreen fronds

wood_rat.n.01.definition: any of several bushy-tailed rodents of the genus Neotoma of western North America; hoards food and other objects

woodsia.n.01.definition: a common rock-inhabiting fern of northern temperate regions having rusty-brown stipes and lanceolate pinnate fronds

word.n.01.definition: the phonological or orthographic sound or appearance of a word that can be used to describe or identify something

work.n.01.definition: any piece of work that is undertaken or attempted

workplace.n.01.definition: a workplace for the conduct of scientific research

work_song.n.01.definition: a rhythmical work song originally sung by sailors

worker.n.01.definition: someone who works (or provides workers) during a strike

workman.n.01.definition: someone who works with their hands; someone engaged in manual labor

worm.n.01.definition: soft unsegmented marine worms that have a threadlike proboscis and the ability to stretch and contract

wormwood.n.01.definition: herb with greyish leaves found along the east coast of North America; used as an ornamental plant

worship.n.01.definition: the second pillar of Islam is prayer; a prescribed liturgy performed five times a day (preferably in a mosque) and oriented toward Mecca

wrestling_hold.n.01.definition: a wrestling hold in which you wrap your legs around the opponents body or head and put your feet together and squeeze

written_record.n.01.definition: a written communication in a second language having the same meaning as the written communication in a first language

x-raying.n.01.definition: a method of examining body organs by scanning them with X rays and using a computer to construct a series of cross-sectional scans along a single axis

xanthoma.n.01.definition: widespread xanthomas (especially on elbows and knees); often associated with a disorder of lipid metabolism

yard.n.09.definition: an enclosed yard for keeping poultry

year.n.03.definition: the time for the earth to make one revolution around the sun, measured between two vernal equinoxes

yellow_pine.n.01.definition: large pine of southern United States having short needles in bunches of 2-3 and red-brown bark when mature

yew.n.02.definition: rare small evergreen of northern Florida; its glossy green leaves have an unpleasant fetid smell when crushed

yucca.n.01.definition: tall arborescent yucca of southwestern United States

zamia.n.01.definition: small tough woody zamia of Florida and West Indies and Cuba; roots and half-buried stems yield an arrowroot

zoonosis.n.01.definition: a highly infectious disease of rodents (especially rabbits and squirrels) and sometimes transmitted to humans by ticks or flies or by handling infected animals
